name: Caliban

distinctive_features: sometimes depicted with fish-like or tortoise features
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: asymmetrical, with exaggerated limbs or features

domain_specific_visual.depiction_variability: can range from wild man to beast man

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: serves as a foil to Prospero and Miranda
domain_specific_functional.symbolic_role: embodies themes of nature vs. nurture

relationships.description: Caliban is the son of the sea witch Sycorax.
Caliban resents Prospero for enslaving him.
Caliban temporarily serves Stephano, mistaking him for a god.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents themes of colonialism and the 'other'

contrastive_constraints: NOT a typical monster without human traits
NOT a culturally specific creature like a werewolf or vampire
